mnemonics.title: OSI Layers Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away - P(Physical) D(Data Link) N(Network) T(Transport) S(Session) P(Presentation) A(Application)

mnemonics.title: TCP/IP Layers
mnemonics.content: TAIP - Transport, Application, Internet, Network Access (link) - remember: T-A-I-N (train)

mnemonics.title: Layer Functions Quick Recall
mnemonics.content: P-DNA-SPA: Physical transmits bits; Data Link frames; Network routes packets; Session syncs; Presentation formats; Application user services

acronyms.usage: Reference model for network communication standardization
Practical implementation model of network protocols
Formatted unit of data at each layer (Segment, Packet, Frame, Bit)
Data passed from one layer to the layer below
Physical address at Data Link layer for local network communication
Logical addressing and routing at Network layer
Application layer protocol for web communication
Application layer protocol for file transfers

visualTips.visualization: Picture a building with 7 floors: Ground floor (Physical) → Parking (Data Link) → Post Office (Network) → Reception (Transport) → Conference Room (Session) → Translation Office (Presentation) → CEO Office (Application). Data 'takes elevator' down, gets wrapped in envelopes at each floor, then goes up and unwraps.

Think of sending a letter: You write content (Application data), put it in an envelope (Transport segment), write destination address (Network packet), put in mailer (Data Link frame), then transmit (Physical bits). Reverse at receiving end.
Each layer only 'talks' to the layer directly above and below it. Like a company hierarchy: CEO talks to managers, managers talk to supervisors, supervisors talk to workers.
Data shrinks like Russian nesting dolls: Application Data → Transport Segment (adds port info) → Network Packet (adds IP) → Data Link Frame (adds MAC) → Physical Bits. Reverse: decapsulation.

analogies.analogy: Like postal system: You write letter (Application), envelope (Transport), address (Network), packaging (Data Link), physical delivery (Physical). Each layer handles its specific job without worrying about others.

Like building a house: Foundation workers (Physical) lay bricks, supervisors (Data Link) organize, architects (Network) plan routes, foremen (Transport) coordinate, project managers (Session) schedule, designers (Presentation) make it look good, client (Application) gets the final house.
Like nested boxes: Each layer adds another box around the data. Recipient opens box by box (decapsulation) to get the original message.
Data Link address is like house number (MAC - physical, local), Network address is like postal code + city (IP - logical, hierarchical for routing).
